headline: PepsiCo and Walmart Face Class Action Alleging Soft Drink Price Coordination
summary: PepsiCo Inc. and Walmart Inc. are facing a proposed nationwide class action lawsuit alleging they coordinated prices for Pepsi-branded soft drinks, leading to higher prices for consumers at retailers other than Walmart. The lawsuit claims PepsiCo sold products to Walmart at lower effective prices and provided unshared promotional support, violating the Sherman Antitrust Act and state laws. Consumers assert these practices constitute vertical price fixing, limiting competition and increasing costs.
